narrative.headline: 2022 Mazda CX-5: exterior lighting tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 61 complaints with NHTSA. Exterior lighting is the most reported area at 36%, followed by the engine (21%) and electrical system (10%). No injuries or deaths are reported. The electrical system stands out for safety with 2 fires and 1 crash, and the brakes, though only 4 reports, are tied to 2 crashes — both worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 22 reports (36%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Check the headlights at night for dim or uneven light.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 13 reports (21%), including 1 fire. Watch for stalling without warning while driving.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 6 reports (10%) but serious by outcome: 2 fires and 1 crash. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 5 reports (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Listen for high RPM or revving with the transmission acting up.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Only 4 reports (7%) but tied to 2 crashes, the highest crash count of any area. Test the brakes thoroughly, including a hard stop.
